title: Leontodon arenicola
taxonomy: Leontodon arenicola is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Leontodon and is also known as the sand dandelion. The plant is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found in sandy areas and grasslands. Its classification at the kingdom level is Plantae, and at the phylum level is Magnoliophyta. The plant's classification at the class level is Magnoliopsida and at the order level is Asterales.

intro: Leontodon arenicola, also known as dune hawk's-bit, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to sandy coastal areas in southern Europe. The health benefits of Leontodon arenicola include its potential to act as an anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agent, which may help in treating various infections and conditions such as wounds and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Leontodon arenicola include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Leontodon arenicola include tea infusions and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
